message:
feat(resolv): port libresolv ns_get/put16/32 + ns_samename / samedomain / subdomain / makecanon (bd-3lw9b)

Add eight low-level libresolv helpers from `<arpa/nameser.h>` that
were missing from our resolv_abi.rs. Programs that LD_PRELOAD
frankenlibc or link against libresolv.so.2 can now resolve them.

Byte read/write (4):
  - ns_get16(*src) -> u16, ns_get32(*src) -> u32 — big-endian load
  - ns_put16(value, *dst), ns_put32(value, *dst) — big-endian store

Name comparators (3):
  - ns_samename(a, b) — case-insensitive ASCII compare with optional
    trailing-dot tolerance ("foo.com" == "foo.com.").
  - ns_samedomain(a, b) — return 1 if a is in b (root "." matches all,
    proper subdomains require a `.` separator before the suffix).
  - ns_subdomain(a, b) — samedomain && !samename.

Canonicalization (1):
  - ns_makecanon(src, dst, dstsiz) — copy with trailing dot ensured;
    sets EMSGSIZE on overflow, EINVAL on NULL.

Tests: 10 abi tests covering big-endian round-trip, NULL tolerance,
case-insensitive match, root-domain semantics, suffix-but-not-subdomain
rejection (e.g., "zzzfoo.com" not in "foo.com"), proper-subdomain
requirement, dot-preservation, and EMSGSIZE / EINVAL paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ku","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Integrate evidence encoding with runtime_math + commitment audit","description":"Wire evidence capture into existing runtime telemetry.

Plan:
- On observe_validation_result(): append systematic evidence records cheaply.
- On cadence (e.g., every 256 obs): generate repair symbols and append.
- Feed commitment_audit with hashes of evidence segments to make tamper-evident.

Acceptance criteria:
- Evidence capture is bounded and does not allocate on hot path.
- Snapshot exports evidence health (loss rate, overhead_percent, last epoch id).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.262792501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:40:54.654618851Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:40:43.345912194Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-1es","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1000,"issue_id":"bd-3ku","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DONE.  {"id":"bd-3kz","title":"Kernel: Alpha-Investing FDR (integrate into RuntimeMathKernel)","description":"Wire alpha-investing into the runtime decision law.

Integration plan:
- decide(): use alpha budget to gate escalation to FullValidate/Repair when multiple monitors are firing.
- observe_validation_result(): feed outcomes back into alpha-wealth updates.
- snapshot(): export wealth/spend/rejections + any per-family state.
- fusion: add severity signal for alpha state if needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Conservative merge: alpha-investing may ONLY escalate or throttle within documented safe bounds (no unsafe de-escalation under high risk).
- Snapshot schema updated additively.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:08.263284738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:38.272517946Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:38.272481989Z","close_reason":"Alpha-Investing fully integrated into RuntimeMathKernel: wired in observe_validation_result() with base_severity input, state cached to atomic, fusion signal[59], 3 snapshot fields (wealth/rejections/fdr). Tests in grobner.rs cover: (1) equivalent_signatures_reduce_to_same_normal_form, (2) reduction_is_idempotent (exhaustive 2^6=64 patterns), (3) confluence_all_64_support_patterns (convergence proof), (4) bounded_runtime_canonical_classification (100k ops < 200ms), (5) reducer_is_o1_with_canonical_table (max steps <= 3), (6) perf_no_degradation_at_large_masks (high-bit independence), (7) canonical_class_covers_all_ids (full coverage). All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:09Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3lw9b","title":"PORT: libresolv ns_get/put16/32 + ns_samename/samedomain/subdomain/makecanon","description":"libresolv ns_get16/ns_get32/ns_put16/ns_put32 (big-endian byte read/write); ns_samename(a, b) (case-insensitive name compare with optional trailing-dot tolerance); ns_samedomain(a, b) (a == b or a ends with .b); ns_subdomain(a, b) (samedomain && !samename); ns_makecanon(src, dst, dstsiz) (copy with trailing dot ensured). Returns 1/0 boolean for the comparators, 0/-1 with errno=EMSGSIZE for makecanon. These are the simplest libresolv parsing helpers and currently absent from our resolv_abi.rs (the high-level resolver lives there but the low-level ns_* helpers do not). Eight functions total.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:13.927262472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:13.927262472Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3mam","title":"bd-1x3 subtask: Workload-ranked top-N API enablement wave planning","description":"Background:
- Stub elimination must be driven by real workload impact and release-level goals.

Goal:
- Define top-N API enablement waves from workload traces and support matrix obligations.

Deliverables:
1) Workload-driven ranking of missing/partial APIs.
2) Wave plan with explicit success criteria and evidence requirements.
3) Integration with existing open subsystem tasks (setjmp/TLS/threading/hard-parts).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Wave order is data-driven and dependency-correct.
- Progress is measurable through objective burn-down metrics.

Verification & Logging:
- Ranking pipeline tests.
- Structured logs for ranking inputs, scores, and wave assignments.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberStone","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.133627294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:23.372513263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:23.372488627Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic workload-ranked top-N API wave plan generator, artifact, gate, and harness test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","roadmap","stubs","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1003,"issue_id":"bd-3mam","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented bd-3mam workload-ranked top-N API enablement wave planning.\n\nDelivered:\n- scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py (new deterministic ranking/wave generator; top-N scoring from workload blockers + support_matrix + callthrough census dependencies)\n- tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json (new generated artifact; top 25 symbol ranking, module ranking, dependency-aware wave plan, integration hooks for setjmp/TLS/threading/hard-parts)\n- scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh (new gate: reproducibility, ranking consistency, acyclic wave dependencies, integration-hook presence, summary consistency, structured log/report emission)\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/workload_api_wave_plan_test.rs (new harness gate test)\n\nVerification:\n- scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh PASS\n- python3 scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py --output tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json --top-n 25 --check PASS\n- python3 -m py_compile scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py PASS\n- bash -n scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh PASS\n- python3 -m json.tool tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json PASS\n\nNote on cargo tests:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test workload_api_wave_plan_test currently blocked by unrelated upstream pthread compile break (E0603 private syscall::raw in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs) outside this bead scope.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-n82j","title":"syscall: sched_setscheduler nonexistent pid with valid param should return ESRCH exactly","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for sched_setscheduler(huge_nonexistent_pid, SCHED_OTHER, &param). +{"id":"bd-nbzrq","title":"PORT: Sun RPC stubs — clnt_*, svc_*, auth*, callrpc, registerrpc (~22 functions)","description":"Add Sun RPC client/server/auth fail-safe stubs to close the remaining glibc T/W parity gaps. The xdr_* family is already implemented (macro-generated), but the RPC framework (clnt_*, svc_*, auth*, callrpc, registerrpc) is not. Real Sun RPC is deprecated and removed from glibc since 2.32 — modern programs use libtirpc. Provide failure-returning stubs so programs that link against these symbols dont fail at link time, and programs that actually try to use RPC get a graceful "no RPC available" result rather than UB. Functions returning pointers (clnt/svc/auth_create variants) return NULL. Functions returning bool/int (svc_register, svc_sendreply, callrpc, registerrpc) return 0 or FALSE. void functions (svc_exit, svc_run, svcerr_*, svc_getreq*, svc_unregister, svcudp_enablecache, clnt_perror) are no-ops. clnt_sperror returns a pointer to a static "rpc unavailable" string so callers that print it dont segfault. ~22 thin stubs total in rpc_abi.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:26:17.096718371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:30:01.327924781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:30:01.325918612Z","close_reason":"false alarm: rpc/svc/clnt/auth fns are already implemented via the rpc_native! macro at lines 2161-2425 of rpc_abi.rs (forwards to glibc dlsym); my naive grep didn't see macro-generated impls; reverted edits, no commit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ndmq","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:12:28.468962613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:34:06.561311256Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:34:06.559034152Z","close_reason":"No repro; io_internal_abi helper already uses stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose and replacement guard passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-ng-1","title":"PORT: netgroup sub-bead 1 — core::netgroup module","description":"Add crates/frankenlibc-core/src/netgroup/mod.rs with NetgroupTriple { host, user, domain }, parse_netgroup_triples(content, group) -> Vec<NetgroupTriple>. Handles inline # comments, whitespace separation, case-insensitive group name match (per glibc files-backend behavior), paren-bounded triples (host,user,domain) with each field trimmed; missing trailing fields default to empty. Tests cover: simple group with multiple triples, empty fields (wildcards), nested whitespace, comments, case-insensitive name match, malformed unclosed paren handled gracefully, group name not found returns empty Vec.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:48:31.965017Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:50:35.123467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:50:35.123467Z","close_reason":"Added crates/frankenlibc-core/src/netgroup/mod.rs with NetgroupTriple { host, user, domain } and parse_netgroup_triples(content, group) -> Vec<NetgroupTriple>. Handles inline # comments, runs of whitespace/tab as separators, case-insensitive group name match, paren-bounded triple extraction with comma split + per-field trim, missing trailing fields default to empty, unclosed paren stops the line gracefully (no panic), bare group references on the same line are skipped (no recursive expansion — matches glibc files-backend). 15 unit tests cover all edge cases including a real-world line and tab-separated input.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-r3qc","title":"syscall: pidfd_send_signal invalid flags should not override invalid pidfd","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1), so invalid pidfd still dominates bad flags. Add ABI regression coverage to freeze that precedence contract exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:01.993657584Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.900227304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.897016719Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1) behavior: invalid flags do not override invalid pidfd, so the veneer still reports exact EBADF; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rcw4p","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid targetfd should return EBADF exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, -1, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-targetfd contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:51.342051427Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.342856887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.339853631Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, -1, 0) → EBADF pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-rdxo4","title":"PORT: __cxa_vec_new/new2/new3 + __cxa_vec_delete/delete2/delete3 + __cxa_vec_cctor","description":"Add the Itanium C++ ABI vector new/delete/copy-ctor family (7 entries) on top of our existing __cxa_vec_ctor/dtor primitives. (1) __cxa_vec_new(count, size, padding, ctor, dtor) -> *mut c_void: malloc(count*size + padding), if padding > 0 stash count at the start of the padding so __cxa_vec_delete can recover it, run ctor on each element, return array (= raw + padding). (2)/(3) __cxa_vec_new2/3 with custom alloc/dealloc functions. (4) __cxa_vec_delete(array, size, padding, dtor): if array NULL no-op; recover raw + count from padding, run dtor reverse, free raw. (5)/(6) __cxa_vec_delete2/3 with custom dealloc (delete2 takes ptr-only, delete3 takes ptr+size). (7) __cxa_vec_cctor(dest, src, count, size, ctor, dtor): copy-constructor variant. NULL ctor/dtor must be tolerated. count*size+padding uses checked arithmetic; overflow returns NULL. Implementations route through our crate::malloc_abi::malloc/free except where the user supplied custom allocators.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:56.551953229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:56.551953229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-rdxo4","title":"PORT: __cxa_vec_new/new2/new3 + __cxa_vec_delete/delete2/delete3 + __cxa_vec_cctor","description":"Add the Itanium C++ ABI vector new/delete/copy-ctor family (7 entries) on top of our existing __cxa_vec_ctor/dtor primitives. (1) __cxa_vec_new(count, size, padding, ctor, dtor) -> *mut c_void: malloc(count*size + padding), if padding > 0 stash count at the start of the padding so __cxa_vec_delete can recover it, run ctor on each element, return array (= raw + padding). (2)/(3) __cxa_vec_new2/3 with custom alloc/dealloc functions. (4) __cxa_vec_delete(array, size, padding, dtor): if array NULL no-op; recover raw + count from padding, run dtor reverse, free raw. (5)/(6) __cxa_vec_delete2/3 with custom dealloc (delete2 takes ptr-only, delete3 takes ptr+size). (7) __cxa_vec_cctor(dest, src, count, size, ctor, dtor): copy-constructor variant. NULL ctor/dtor must be tolerated. count*size+padding uses checked arithmetic; overflow returns NULL. Implementations route through our crate::malloc_abi::malloc/free except where the user supplied custom allocators.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:56.551953229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.929936674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.927873908Z","close_reason":"shipped: 7 cxa vector new/delete/cctor primitives w/ count-in-padding stashing + 8 abi tests including round-trip and custom alloc/dealloc, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host).  {"id":"bd-reax.1","title":"Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.021445416Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.020480090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-reax.2","title":"Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.515692509Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.514670096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.2","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
